content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662           322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

August 21, 1981


Ms. Susie Lange
Assistant to the Director
Department of Forestry
1416 Ninth Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
                                                    A-81-025-0417

                                        Re:  Your Letter of
                                             August 11, 1981, to
                                             Jim McManus

Dear Ms. Lange:

     Your letter has been referred to the Legal
Division for a response.  The facts, as you state
them, are as follows:

     "The California Department of
Forestry is currently contracting with
Solem and Associates and Vis/Arts
Films for a major mass media fire
prevention program.  We have jointly
produced and circulated a number of
radio and television public service
announcements which are now being
released.

     During the taping of one of the
television spots in Los Angeles, Assemblyman
Art Torres was contacted, and volunteered
to do a thirty second spot in Spanish.
It follows the same theme as the major
campaign: 'Wildfires, We can't
stop them without you!' and stresses
what individuals can do around their
dwellings, as well as while in the
wild land, to prevent fires."

Ms. Susie Lange
August 21, 1981
Page Two


     In response to your questions: First, broadcasting
the Torres spot is permissible; Second, there is no
limitation as to where you may broadcast it. Our
regulations, and the Political Reform Act, would
become relevant only if the spot were campaign related.
The facts related above indicate that clearly it is
not.

     If you have any questions, please feel free to
contact me at 322-5750.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Ledigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:ce

STATE OF CALIFORNIA—RESOURCES AGENCY                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY
1416 NINTH STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814

Telephone: 916-445-9144
                                                                        August 11, 1981
                                                                        L1




Mr. Jim McManus
Chief Investigator
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. McManus:

     The California Department of Forestry is currently contracting with Solem and
Associates and Vis/Arts Films for a major mass media fire prevention program.
We have jointly produced and circulated a number of radio and television public
service announcements which are now being released.

     During the taping of one of the television spots in Los Angeles, Assemblyman
Art Torres was contacted, and volunteered to do a thirty second spot in Spanish.
It follows the same theme as the major campaign: "Wildfires, We can't stop them
without you!" and stresses what individuals can do around their dwellings, as
well as while in the wildland, to prevent fires.

     We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting
the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would
be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district
or broadcasting area.

     If you have any further questions, or would like to see the spot, please call
me at 445-9144.

                                                    Sincerely,

                                                    [signature]
                                                    Susie Lange
                                                    Assistant to the Director

sd


CONSERVATION IS WISE USE — KEEP CALIFORNIA GREEN AND GOLDEN

sections.question: We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district or broadcasting area.
sections.question_synthetic: May the California Department of Forestry broadcast a public service announcement featuring Assemblyman Art Torres, and are there geographic restrictions on broadcasting such an announcement based on his district?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the Department of Forestry to FPPC Chief Investigator. The document ID 81A047 and year 1981 appear to reference the FPPC's response document, but the provided text contains primarily the original request letter. The document contains some OCR artifacts and scanning quality issues that slightly affect confidence. No formal CONCLUSION or FPPC response section is present in the provided text.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district or broadcasting area.

BROWN JR., Governor DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY 1416 NINTH STREET SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 Telephone: 916-445-9144 August 11, 1981 L1 Mr. Jim McManus Chief Investigator Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 Dear Mr. McManus: The California Department of Forestry is currently contracting with Solem and Associates and Vis/Arts Films for a major mass media fire prevention program. We have jointly produced and circulated a number of radio and television public service announcements which are now being released. During the taping of one of the television spots in Los Angeles, Assemblyman Art Torres was contacted, and volunteered to do a thirty second spot in Spanish. It follows the same theme as the major campaign: "Wildfires, We can't stop them without you!" and stresses what individuals can do around their dwellings, as well as while in the wildland, to prevent fires. We now need an informal opinion, or a technical ruling, on whether broadcasting the Torres spot is permissible under FPPC regulations, and/or whether we would be limited to broadcasting it outside of the assemblyman's immediate district or broadcasting area. If you have any further questions, or would like to see the spot, please call me at 445-9144. Sincerely, [signature] Susie Lange Assistant to the Director sd CONSERVATION
embedding.summary: The California Department of Forestry requests an FPPC opinion regarding whether they may broadcast a fire prevention public service announcement featuring Assemblyman Art Torres without geographic restrictions based on his legislative district.
